verses.latin_vulgate: Onus verbi Domini ad Israël in manu Malachiæ.
verses.douay_rheims: The burden of the word of the Lord to Israel by the hand of Malachi.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on Herran sanan kuorma Israelin tykö, Malakian kautta.
verses.king_james: The burden of the word of the Lord to Israel by Malachi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dilexi vos, dicit Dominus, et dixistis : In quo dilexisti nos ? Nonne frater erat Esau Jacob ? dicit Dominus : et dilexi Jacob,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָהַ֤בְתִּי אֶתְכֶם֙ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֖ם בַּמָּ֣ה אֲהַבְתָּ֑נוּ הֲלוֹא־אָ֨ח עֵשָׂ֤ו לְיַֽעֲקֹב֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה וָאֹהַ֖ב אֶֽת־יַעֲקֹֽב׃
verses.douay_rheims: I have loved you, saith the Lord: and you have said: Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau brother to Jacob, saith the Lord, and I have loved Jacob,
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä rakastin teitä, sanoo Herra. Niin te sanotte: missäs meitä rakastit? Eikö Esau ole Jakobin veli, sanoo Herra? ja minä rakastin Jakobia,
verses.king_james: I have loved you, saith the Lord. Yet ye say, Wherein hast thou loved us? Was not Esau Jacob’s brother? saith the Lord: yet I loved Jacob,

verses.latin_vulgate: Esau autem odio habui, et posui montes ejus in solitudinem, et hæreditatem ejus in dracones deserti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־עֵשָׂ֖ו שָׂנֵ֑אתִי וָאָשִׂ֤ים אֶת־הָרָיו֙ שְׁמָמָ֔ה וְאֶת־נַחֲלָת֖וֹ לְתַנּ֥וֹת מִדְבָּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: But have hated Esau? and I have made his mountains a wilderness, and given his inheritance to the dragons of the desert.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vihasin Esauta; olen myös autioksi tehnyt hänen vuorensa, ja hänen perimisensä lohikärmeille erämaaksi.
verses.king_james: And I hated Esau, and laid his mountains and his heritage waste for the dragons of the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod si dixerit Idumæa : Destructi sumus, sed revertentes ædificabimus quæ destructa sunt : hæc dicit Dominus exercituum : Isti ædificabunt, et ego destruam : et vocabuntur termini impietatis, et populus cui iratus est Dominus usque in æternum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־תֹאמַ֨ר אֱד֜וֹם רֻשַּׁ֗שְׁנוּ וְנָשׁוּב֙ וְנִבְנֶ֣ה חֳרָב֔וֹת כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת הֵ֥מָּה יִבְנ֖וּ וַאֲנִ֣י אֶהֱר֑וֹס וְקָרְא֤וּ לָהֶם֙ גְּב֣וּל רִשְׁעָ֔ה וְהָעָ֛ם אֲשֶׁר־זָעַ֥ם יְהֹוָ֖ה עַד־עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: But if Edom shall say: We are destroyed, but we will return and build up what hath been destroyed: thus saith the Lord of hosts: They shall build up, and I will throw down: and they shall be called the borders of wickedness, and the people with whom the Lord is angry for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja jos Edom sanois: me olemme köyhtyneet, mutta me tahdomme autiot jälleen rakentaa; niin sanoo Herra Zebaot näin: jos he rakentavat, niin minä tahdon maahan kukistaa; ja ne pitää kutsuttaman jumalattomuuden rajaksi ja siksi kansaksi, jolle Herra on vihainen ijankaikkisesti.
verses.king_james: Whereas Edom saith, We are impoverished, but we will return and build the desolate places; thus saith the Lord of hosts, They shall build, but I will throw down; and they shall call them, The border of wickedness, and, The people against whom the Lord hath indignation for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et oculi vestri videbunt, et vos dicetis : Magnificetur Dominus super terminum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעֵינֵיכֶ֖ם תִּרְאֶ֑ינָה וְאַתֶּ֤ם תֹּֽאמְרוּ֙ יִגְדַּ֣ל יְהֹוָ֔ה מֵעַ֖ל לִגְב֥וּל יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And your eyes shall see: and you shall say: The Lord be magnified upon the border of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen pitää teidän silmänne näkemän, ja teidän pitää sanoman: Herra on ylistetty Israelin maan äärissä.
verses.king_james: And your eyes shall see, and ye shall say, The Lord will be magnified from the border of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filius honorat patrem, et servus dominum suum. Si ergo pater ego sum, ubi est honor meus ? et si Dominus ego sum, ubi est timor meus ? dicit Dominus exercituum. Ad vos, o sacerdotes, qui despicitis nomen meum, et dixistis : In quo despeximus nomen tuum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּ֛ן יְכַבֵּ֥ד אָ֖ב וְעֶ֣בֶד אֲדֹנָ֑יו וְאִם־אָ֣ב אָ֣נִי אַיֵּ֣ה כְבוֹדִ֡י וְאִם־אֲדוֹנִ֣ים אָ֩נִי֩ אַיֵּ֨ה מוֹרָאִ֜י אָמַ֣ר ׀ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֗וֹת לָכֶם֙ הַכֹּֽהֲנִים֙ בּוֹזֵ֣י שְׁמִ֔י וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֕ם בַּמֶּ֥ה בָזִ֖ינוּ אֶת־שְׁמֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The son honoureth the father, and the servant his master: if then I be a father, where is my honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear: saith the Lord of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pojan pitää kunnioittaman isäänsä, ja palvelian isäntäänsä. Jos minä siis isä olen, kussa on minun kunniani? Jos minä isäntä olen, kussa on minun pelkoni? sanoo Herra Zebaot teille, papit, jotka katsotte ylön minun nimeni. Niin te sanotte: missä me katsomme ylön sinun nimes?
verses.king_james: A son honoureth his father, and a servant his master: if then I be a father, where is mine honour? and if I be a master, where is my fear? saith the Lord of hosts unto you, O priests, that despise my name. And ye say, Wherein have we despised thy name?

verses.latin_vulgate: Offertis super altare meum panem pollutum, et dicitis : In quo polluimus te ? in eo quod dicitis : Mensa Domini despecta est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַגִּישִׁ֤ים עַֽל־מִזְבְּחִי֙ לֶ֣חֶם מְגֹאָ֔ל וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֖ם בַּמֶּ֣ה גֵאַלְנ֑וּךָ בֶּאֱמׇרְכֶ֕ם שֻׁלְחַ֥ן יְהֹוָ֖ה נִבְזֶ֥ה הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: To you, O priests, that despise my name, and have said: Wherein have we despised thy name? You offer polluted bread upon my altar, and you say: Wherein have we polluted thee? In that you say: The table of the Lord is contemptible.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin kuin te uhraatte minun alttarilleni saastaista leipää. Niin te sanotte: kuinka me uhraamme sinulle saastaista? Siinä että te sanotte: Herran pöytä on katsottu ylön.
verses.king_james: Ye offer polluted bread upon mine altar; and ye say, Wherein have we polluted thee? In that ye say, The table of the Lord is contemptible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si offeratis cæcum ad immolandum, nonne malum est ? et si offeratis claudum et languidum, nonne malum est ? offer illud duci tuo, si placuerit ei, aut si susceperit faciem tuam, dicit Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכִי־תַגִּישׁ֨וּן עִוֵּ֤ר לִזְבֹּ֙חַ֙ אֵ֣ין רָ֔ע וְכִ֥י תַגִּ֛ישׁוּ פִּסֵּ֥חַ וְחֹלֶ֖ה אֵ֣ין רָ֑ע הַקְרִיבֵ֨הוּ נָ֜א לְפֶחָתֶ֗ךָ הֲיִרְצְךָ֙ א֚וֹ הֲיִשָּׂ֣א פָנֶ֔יךָ אָמַ֖ר יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: If you offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if you offer the lame and the sick, is it not evil? offer it to thy prince, if he will be pleased with it, or if he will regard thy face, saith the Lord of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin te uhraatte jotakin, joka sokia on, niin ette sano sitä pahaksi; ja kuin te uhraatte jotakin ontuvaa eli sairasta, niin ei sitäkään sanota pahaksi. Vie senkaltaista sinun päämiehilles, mitämaks, jos sinä olet hänelle otollinen, eli jos hän katsoo sinun puolees, sanoo Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: And if ye offer the blind for sacrifice, is it not evil? and if ye offer the lame and sick, is it not evil? offer it now unto thy governor; will he be pleased with thee, or accept thy person? saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc deprecamini vultum Dei ut misereatur vestri (de manu enim vestra factum est hoc), si quomodo suscipiat facies vestras, dicit Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֛ה חַלּוּ־נָ֥א פְנֵי־אֵ֖ל וִֽיחׇנֵּ֑נוּ מִיֶּדְכֶם֙ הָ֣יְתָה זֹּ֔את הֲיִשָּׂ֤א מִכֶּם֙ פָּנִ֔ים אָמַ֖ר יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And now beseech ye the face of God, that he may have mercy on you, (for by your hand hath this been done,) if by any means he will receive your faces, saith the Lord of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin rukoilkaat nyt Jumalaa, että hän armahtais meidän päällemme, sillä se on tapahtunut teiltä. Luuletteko, että hän katsoo teidän puoleenne? sanoo Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: And now, I pray you, beseech God that he will be gracious unto us: this hath been by your means: will he regard your persons? saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis est in vobis qui claudat ostia, et incendat altare meum gratuito ? non est mihi voluntas in vobis, dicit Dominus exercituum, et munus non suscipiam de manu vestra.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִ֤י גַם־בָּכֶם֙ וְיִסְגֹּ֣ר דְּלָתַ֔יִם וְלֹא־תָאִ֥ירוּ מִזְבְּחִ֖י חִנָּ֑ם אֵֽין־לִ֨י חֵ֜פֶץ בָּכֶ֗ם אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת וּמִנְחָ֖ה לֹא־אֶרְצֶ֥ה מִיֶּדְכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who is there among you, that will shut the doors, and will kindle the fire on my altar gratis? I have no pleasure in you, saith the Lord of hosts: and I will not receive a gift of your hand.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuka on myös teidän seassanne, joka sulkee oven (ilman palkkaa)? ette myös viritä tulta minun alttarilleni ilman mitäkään. Ei minulla ole suosiota teihin, sanoo Herra Zebaot, ja ruokauhri teidän käsistänne ei ole minulle otollinen.
verses.king_james: Who is there even among you that would shut the doors for nought? neither do ye kindle fire on mine altar for nought. I have no pleasure in you, saith the Lord of hosts, neither will I accept an offering at your hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ab ortu enim solis usque ad occasum, magnum est nomen meum in gentibus, et in omni loco sacrificatur : et offertur nomini meo oblatio munda, quia magnum est nomen meum in gentibus, dicit Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י מִמִּזְרַח־שֶׁ֜מֶשׁ וְעַד־מְבוֹא֗וֹ גָּד֤וֹל שְׁמִי֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם וּבְכׇל־מָק֗וֹם מֻקְטָ֥ר מֻגָּ֛שׁ לִשְׁמִ֖י וּמִנְחָ֣ה טְהוֹרָ֑ה כִּֽי־גָד֤וֹל שְׁמִי֙ בַּגּוֹיִ֔ם אָמַ֖ר יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: For from the rising of the sun even to the going down, my name is great among the Gentiles, and in every place there is sacrifice, and there is offered to my name a clean oblation: for my name is great among the Gentiles, saith the Lord of hosts.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan hamasta auringon koitosta niin laskemiseen asti pitää minun nimeni suureksi tuleman pakanain seassa, ja jokapaikassa pitää minun nimelleni suitsutettaman, ja puhdas ruokauhri uhrattaman; sillä minun nimeni pitää suureksi tuleman pakanain seassa, sanoo Herra Zebaot.
verses.king_james: For from the rising of the sun even unto the going down of the same my name shall be great among the Gentiles; and in every place incense shall be offered unto my name, and a pure offering: for my name shall be great among the heathen, saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vos polluistis illud in eo quod dicitis : Mensa Domini contaminata est, et quod superponitur contemptibilis est, cum igne qui illud devorat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתֶּ֖ם מְחַלְּלִ֣ים אוֹת֑וֹ בֶּאֱמׇרְכֶ֗ם שֻׁלְחַ֤ן אֲדֹנָי֙ מְגֹאָ֣ל ה֔וּא וְנִיב֖וֹ נִבְזֶ֥ה אׇכְלֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And you have profaned it in that you say: The table of the Lord is defiled: and that which is laid thereupon is contemptible with the fire that devoureth it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta te turmelette sen sillä, kuin te sanotte: Herran pöytä on saastutettu, ja sen uhri on katsottu ylön ja hänen ruokansa.
verses.king_james: But ye have profaned it, in that ye say, The table of the Lord is polluted; and the fruit thereof, even his meat, is contemptible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixistis : Ecce de labore, et exsufflastis illud, dicit Dominus exercituum : et intulistis de rapinis claudum et languidum, et intulistis munus : numquid suscipiam illud de manu vestra ? dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲמַרְתֶּם֩ הִנֵּ֨ה מַתְּלָאָ֜ה וְהִפַּחְתֶּ֣ם אוֹת֗וֹ אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת וַהֲבֵאתֶ֣ם גָּז֗וּל וְאֶת־הַפִּסֵּ֙חַ֙ וְאֶת־הַ֣חוֹלֶ֔ה וַהֲבֵאתֶ֖ם אֶת־הַמִּנְחָ֑ה הַאֶרְצֶ֥ה אוֹתָ֛הּ מִיֶּדְכֶ֖ם אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And you have said: Behold of our labour, and you puffed it away, saith the Lord of hosts, and you brought in of rapine the lame, and the sick, and brought in an offering: shall I accept it at your hands, saith the Lord?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja te sanotte: katso, se on sula vaiva, ja lyötte sen tuleen, sanoo Herra Zebaot. Ja te uhraatte ryövättyä, ontuvaa ja sairasta, ja ne te uhraatte ruokauhriksi. Pitäiskö se minulle otollinen oleman teidän käsistänne? sanoo Herra.
verses.king_james: Ye said also, Behold, what a weariness is it! and ye have snuffed at it, saith the Lord of hosts; and ye brought that which was torn, and the lame, and the sick; thus ye brought an offering: should I accept this of your hand? saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maledictus dolosus qui habet in grege suo masculum, et votum faciens immolat debile Domino : quia rex magnus ego, dicit Dominus exercituum, et nomen meum horribile in gentibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָר֣וּר נוֹכֵ֗ל וְיֵ֤שׁ בְּעֶדְרוֹ֙ זָכָ֔ר וְנֹדֵ֛ר וְזֹבֵ֥חַ מׇשְׁחָ֖ת לַאדֹנָ֑י כִּי֩ מֶ֨לֶךְ גָּד֜וֹל אָ֗נִי אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת וּשְׁמִ֖י נוֹרָ֥א בַגּוֹיִֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Cursed is the deceitful man that hath in his flock a male, and making a vow offereth in sacrifice that which is feeble to the Lord: for I am a great King, saith the Lord of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the Gentiles.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kirottu olkoon pettäjä, jonka laumassa on oinas, ja kuin hän tekee lupauksen, niin hän uhraa Herralle kelvottoman; sillä minä olen suuri kuningas, sanoo Herra Zebaot, ja minun nimeni on peljättävä pakanain seassa.
verses.king_james: But cursed be the deceiver, which hath in his flock a male, and voweth, and sacrificeth unto the Lord a corrupt thing: for I am a great King, saith the Lord of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the heathen.
